test/notification.purpose: Integration test suite for the notification domain: channels, rules, events, delivery status, webhook delivery via Svix, and the balance-threshold consumer handler. Wires the full notification stack including a real Svix client from environment variables via TestEnv.

patterns.name: TestEnv interface for service access
patterns.description: All test suites receive a TestEnv value and call env.Notification(), env.NotificationRepo(), env.Feature(), env.Meter(), env.NotificationWebhook() instead of touching service structs directly. Created via NewTestEnv(t, ctx, namespace) and closed via env.Close().
patterns.example: env, err := NewTestEnv(t, ctx, namespace); service := env.Notification()

patterns.name: Sub-suite structs with Setup + named test methods
patterns.description: Behaviour splits into XxxTestSuite structs (ChannelTestSuite, RuleTestSuite, EventTestSuite, RepositoryTestSuite, WebhookTestSuite, BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite), each with an optional Setup. Top-level TestNotification constructs, sets up, and runs subtests.
patterns.example: testSuite := ChannelTestSuite{Env: env}; t.Run("Create", func(t *testing.T) { testSuite.TestCreate(ctx, t) })

patterns.name: Svix from SVIX_HOST env var
patterns.description: testenv.go reads SVIX_HOST (default 127.0.0.1) and SVIX_JWT_SECRET to connect to a real Svix instance. Webhook delivery assertions require docker compose up -d svix or SVIX_HOST set.

patterns.name: NewBalanceSnapshotEvent for balance consumer tests
patterns.description: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite uses NewBalanceSnapshotEvent(BalanceSnapshotEventInput{...}) to build synthetic snapshot.SnapshotEvent values passed to handler.Handle(ctx, event).
patterns.example: snapshotEvent := NewBalanceSnapshotEvent(BalanceSnapshotEventInput{Feature: s.feature, Value: snapshot.EntitlementValue{Balance: convert.ToPointer(50.0)}, Namespace: s.namespace})

patterns.name: setupNamespace for isolated balance handler tests
patterns.description: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite.setupNamespace creates a fresh ULID namespace, replaces meters, creates feature/channel/rule, and constructs consumer.EntitlementSnapshotHandler. Called inside each TestXxx, not a shared Setup.

patterns.name: eventHandler started as goroutine, closed via env.Close()
patterns.description: testenv.go runs eventHandler.Start() in a goroutine inside NewTestEnv; the closerFunc calls eventHandler.Close() then closes Ent/PG drivers. Always defer env.Close() or use t.Cleanup.

key_file_guides.role: Boots the full notification stack: Ent adapter, real Svix webhook handler, pglockx lock client, event handler goroutine, notification service, feature connector, and meter mock adapter. Returns closerFunc.
key_file_guides.watch_for: eventHandler.Start() runs in a goroutine — must call env.Close(). Svix connectivity required for webhook delivery tests. TestEnv fields are set only after NewTestEnv returns.

key_file_guides.role: BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite and helpers; tests multi-step threshold crossing and reset deduplication via direct handler.Handle calls (not Kafka).
key_file_guides.watch_for: TestEntitlementCurrentUsagePeriod is a package-level var computed at init (time.Now()); tests depending on exact period boundaries may be flaky near boundaries.

key_file_guides.role: Top-level TestNotification entry point: creates one TestEnv, constructs all sub-suites, calls Setup, runs subtests.
key_file_guides.watch_for: All sub-suites share the same TestEnv and namespace — data created in one suite is visible to others. Use distinct IDs/names.

key_file_guides.role: RepositoryTestSuite tests repo-layer filter methods (ListEvents Features/Subjects filters) directly via env.NotificationRepo(), bypassing the service layer for precise filter testing.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Uses context.Background() in TestFilterEventByFeature/BySubject — acceptable for pure repo-filter tests without Ent tx propagation needs.

key_file_guides.role: Stateless helpers: NewSvixAuthToken (HS256 JWT for Svix API auth) and NewClickhouseClient (present but unused).
key_file_guides.watch_for: NewClickhouseClient exists for future use; notification tests do not currently touch ClickHouse directly.

anti_patterns: Running webhook delivery assertions without a live Svix instance — tests fail if SVIX_HOST is unset and assertions hit the Svix API.
Creating a second TestEnv inside a test method — each starts a goroutine and opens DB connections; reuse the suite-level env.
Forgetting defer env.Close() — leaves the eventHandler goroutine and DB/Ent connections open.
Using package-level time vars (TestEntitlementCurrentUsagePeriod) as exact assertion values in time-sensitive tests — computed at package init.
Calling env.Notification() or env.NotificationRepo() before NewTestEnv completes — fields are only set after it returns.

decisions.decision: TestEnv interface rather than exposing service structs directly.
decisions.rationale: Decouples suites from concrete wiring in testenv.go; allows swapping Svix for a mock without modifying every test file.

decisions.decision: Wire a real Svix client (not a mock) in testenv.go.
decisions.rationale: Notification correctness depends on Svix application/endpoint/message creation semantics a mock cannot catch; gated by SVIX_HOST availability.

code_examples.scenario: Add a new balance-consumer test step to BalanceNotificaiontHandlerTestSuite

test/subscription.purpose: Integration test suite for subscription lifecycle scenarios — verifying the full billing+sync stack (plan creation, subscription create/edit/cancel, billing sync, invoice line generation) against a real PostgreSQL database via pgtestdb. Tests are scenario-based and exercise cross-domain flows that unit tests cannot cover.

patterns.name: Single shared setup() via testDeps
patterns.description: All tests call setup(t, setupConfig{}) which wires every dependency from scratch via direct constructors (no app/common imports). The returned testDeps embeds subscriptiontestutils.SubscriptionDependencies plus billing, sync, app services. Always defer tDeps.cleanup(t).

patterns.name: clock.SetTime for deterministic time control
patterns.description: Tests set a fixed start via testutils.GetRFC3339Time then advance with clock.SetTime(...). All services use pkg/clock, so this controls all time-dependent billing logic.

patterns.name: Numbered step comments for scenario readability
patterns.description: Test bodies use numbered comments (// 1st, // 2nd, ...) documenting the causal sequence: feature → plan → publish → customer → subscription → mutations → assertions.
patterns.example: // 1st, create features ... // 2nd, create plan ... // 4th, create subscription

patterns.name: Plan published before subscription creation
patterns.description: Every scenario calls PlanService.PublishPlan with EffectiveFrom before pcSubscriptionService.Create. An unpublished plan causes subscription creation to fail.
patterns.example: p, err = tDeps.PlanService.PublishPlan(ctx, plan.PublishPlanInput{NamespacedID: p.NamespacedID, EffectivePeriod: productcatalog.EffectivePeriod{EffectiveFrom: lo.ToPtr(currentTime)}})

patterns.name: minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate for billing profile setup
patterns.description: Tests exercising billing call minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate(tDeps.sandboxApp.GetID()) (in framework_test.go) for a baseline profile with AlignmentKindSubscription and PT0S collection interval, then override before CreateProfile.

patterns.name: Hardcoded test-namespace
patterns.description: All tests use namespace := "test-namespace", hardcoded in subscriptiontestutils.SetupDBDeps. Do not use any other namespace string.

patterns.name: Direct constructor wiring, no app/common imports
patterns.description: framework_test.go builds billingservice, billingadapter, subscriptionsyncservice, appservice, etc. via their New(Config{...}) constructors directly. Importing app/common creates import cycles.

key_file_guides.role: Defines testDeps struct, setup(), and minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate. All scenario files share package subscription_test. Registers appsandbox.NewMockableFactory as a side-effect before billing use.
key_file_guides.watch_for: A new service in setup() needs a matching testDeps field; forgetting cleanup(t) leaks the pgtestdb database; the sandbox app must be registered via appsandbox.NewMockableFactory before billing wiring.

key_file_guides.role: Tests that editing a running subscription preserves the metered entitlement's current usage period after PatchRemoveItem + PatchAddItem.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Assertions compare ItemsByKey[key][0] (old/expired) vs ItemsByKey[key][1] (new replacement) — index 1 is the replacement entitlement.

key_file_guides.role: Tests editing then immediately canceling; also stress-tests with 10 parallel customers to surface concurrency issues.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Extra customers use unique subject keys (subject_2..subject_11); duplicate subject keys are rejected by customer service.

key_file_guides.role: Regression test for subscription creation with a metered entitlement in the second phase (not the first). Does NOT create a billing profile.
key_file_guides.watch_for: 'THIS IS THE TEST, it used to fail' marks the key assertion. Do not add billing assertions here without first creating a profile.

key_file_guides.role: Tests billing anchor alignment (in-arrears usage, in-advance flat fee, daily cadence ratecards) aligning to first-of-month. Calls subscriptionSyncService.SynchronizeSubscription directly.
key_file_guides.watch_for: Line count depends on days between startOfSub and endOfMonth; assertions group via lo.GroupBy and sort by period start — fragile to GetRFC3339Time date changes.

anti_patterns: Importing app/common or any Wire provider set — creates import cycles with domain testutils packages.
Using context.Background() where t.Context() is available — the harness must own context cancellation.
Using a namespace other than 'test-namespace' — hardcoded in SetupDBDeps infrastructure.
Calling subscriptionSyncService or billingService without first creating a billing profile — profile is required for invoice generation.
Mutating clock via clock.SetTime without restoring after the test — leaked global clock state affects other tests.

decisions.decision: Scenario files rather than table-driven tests.
decisions.rationale: Each scenario exercises a different causal sequence varying in setup, timing, and assertions; table-driven structure would obscure the temporal flow central to billing correctness.

decisions.decision: All wiring via direct constructors in framework_test.go, not app/common Wire sets.
decisions.rationale: Avoids import cycles between domain testutils and the application wiring layer; keeps dependencies minimal and explicit.

decisions.decision: Shared testDeps with embedded SubscriptionDependencies rather than per-test setup structs.
decisions.rationale: Each test calls setup() independently (fresh pgtestdb DB), but the shared struct shape lets scenario files access all services without re-declaring them.

code_examples.scenario: Standard scenario: setup deps, control clock, create feature+plan+customer, subscribe, advance time, assert
code_examples.code:
func TestMyScenario(t *testing.T) {
	namespace := "test-namespace"
	currentTime := testutils.GetRFC3339Time(t, "2025-06-15T12:00:00Z")
	ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(t.Context())
	defer cancel()
	tDeps := setup(t, setupConfig{})
	defer tDeps.cleanup(t)
	clock.SetTime(currentTime)
	_, err := tDeps.billingService.CreateProfile(ctx, minimalCreateProfileInputTemplate(tDeps.sandboxApp.GetID()))
	require.NoError(t, err)
	// 1st, create feature ...
}
